plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-449 RP14.P08.M07.S02-RP14.P08.M08.S19 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-449 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP14.P08 test hermes slice: the M07 Test And Golden Case Set tail (the twenty-one remaining rows) and the M08 Hermes Evidence Packet head (the first nineteen rows) as two descriptor-only sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H14), Claude review packet (C14), and CP00-450 handoff, without opening settlement, origination, allocation, or working credit runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-449 is a descriptor-only RP14.P08 test/hermes slice (M07 Test And Golden Case Set tail S02-S22 = 21 rows, plus M08 Hermes Evidence Packet head S01-S19 = 19 rows), risk class B, 40 units. The pack-level claim is conservative and supported: manifest.json (production_ready=false:6, implementation_layer=descriptor:8, runtime_ready=false:9) and command-evidence.json (production_ready=false:6, writes_product_state=false:8) make no runtime/production assertion; the only positive claim is the descriptor-verified flag settlement_core_p08_test_hermes_slice_descriptor_verified. Pack binding is mutually consistent across registry.js:392-407 (SETTLEMENT_CORE_CP449_PACK_BINDING) and :2129-2171 (requirements), the contract current_pack (settlement-core-contract.json:17-31), cp449_requirements (4665), p08_test_hermes_slice_descriptor projection (26588 -> SettlementCoreCp449P08TestHermesSliceDescriptor) and historical_pack_bindings tail (27588), the manifest plan_binding_snapshot, the review baseline, the live closeout-pack-plan.json:410, and next-pack-queue.json (live_next_unit_id RP14.P08.M07.S02:9) — all matching pack_id CP00-449, range RP14.P08.M07.S02-RP14.P08.M08.S19, upstream CP00-448, next CP00-450 / RP14.P08.M08.S20. All counts reconcile: 40 units; deliverables {hermes_evidence 19, claude_review 2, implementation 17, test 2}; micro phases {M07 21, M08 19}; micro titles {Test And Golden Case Set 21, Hermes Evidence Packet 19} — confirmed in registry requirements, validateSettlementCoreCp449Coverage (validators.js:4180-4213), and the baseline included_units. The seven required public exports exist (service.js:2963/2996; validators.js:4180/4215/4271/4292/4315) and are re-exported via index.js:1-3 (export *). Changed source is pure and side-effect-free: grep found no fs/child_process/exec/process.env/network usage in packages/settlement/src; freezeCp449Result (service.js:2915-2957) and freezeCp449Validation (validators.js:4158-4164) return Object.frozen structures asserting writes_product_state=false, no settlement/origination/allocation/working-credit runtime, no audit/state/DB/object-storage writes, no Hermes runtime receipt, no command runtime, no real fixture data, and no payload/permission/audit/blocked-claim leaks. Authority boundary holds: descriptor authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false and local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false (service.js:3008-3013), enforced by validators.js:4247-4254 and asserted in model.test.js:1613/1630-1631 and the contract validator. Hermes/Claude patterns are used only for the descriptor-only review packet/receipt shape (receipt_shape descriptor_only_settlement_p08_test_hermes_slice_no_write, service.js:3014-3026), not product runtime (manifest no_hermes_runtime_receipt=true). Tests cover the pack in three non-skipped cases (model.test.js:1583-1632; grep found no .only/.skip/it.todo), and the contract validator adds CP449 assertions (scripts/validate-rp14-settlement-core-contract.mjs:320-321, 371-372, 535-541, 1314, 1322). Readiness auth preflight passed cleanly (readiness-report.json: status passed, exit 0, stdout 1456 bytes of valid JSON result) — no auth failure, zero-byte, malformed, fenced-with-prose, or tool-call-shaped output; validation-report.json confirms read-only tools only and forbidden_tools_present=false. Changed-file-scope correctly isolates unrelated dirty files (docx, workbook/*, ldip) from the active settlement refs. Note on convention: the input authority_boundary carries enterprise_trust_claim_closed=true ("closed outside this receipt" per review-packet.md), which the downstream normalize/adjudication step flips to false in claude-review-result.json — identical to the just-closed CP00-448, so it is the established pattern, not a defect. Two P3 informational notes only: (1) post-review artifacts claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json are not yet present, expected because the hardening_sequence produces them after this read-only run and they must exist and validate before commit; (2) the command-evidence exit codes are self-reported and cannot be re-executed under read-only tools, so this PASS attests artifact consistency and source support, deferring execution truth to the deterministic control-plane gates and human final approval. No P0/P1/P2 issues; the pack blocks neither pack-level nor goal-level closeout, Claude remains a read-only reviewer and not final approver, and enterprise-trust final approval remains a human responsibility outside this receipt.

findings.title: Post-review required artifacts not yet present (expected at this stage)
findings.details: manifest.required_pack_artifacts (and evidence_refs:1669) list claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, which are absent from docs/closeout-packs/cp00-449/ at this read-only review stage (Glob returns only manifest.json and command-evidence.json). Per manifest.pack_level_claude_review.hardening_sequence (1732-1742), these are produced by later steps (normalize -> finding_adjudication -> construction_inspection) that consume this review's output, so their absence now is expected and not a defect. The same artifacts are present for the just-closed CP00-448, confirming the sequence.
findings.recommendation: Ensure all three artifacts are generated from the accepted review and pass receipt/final-closeout validation before commit; do not close the pack until they exist and validate (raw-output.json/review-receipt.json also not yet written, consistent with this stage).

findings.title: Command-evidence exit codes are self-reported, not re-executed in read-only review
findings.details: All recorded commands (node --check/--test, the rp05-rp14 core validators, closeout-pack-plan/weighted/spec/control-plane/goal/fullplan validate, npm test, npm run validate, npm run build, git diff --check) report exit_code 0, but under the read-only tool set (Read/Grep/Glob, no Bash) these cannot be independently re-executed. Static inspection corroborates the descriptor-only, no-write, and no-real-data claims, and model.test.js contains non-skipped CP00-449 coverage (plan binding/coverage, descriptor-only rows, evidence packet/handoff authority boundaries), so the recorded evidence is internally consistent with the source.
findings.recommendation: Keep execution verification gated by the deterministic control-plane (closeout-pack receipt/normalize/validate and final closeout validation) plus human final approval; treat this read-only PASS as attesting artifact consistency and source support, not independent command re-execution.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-449 settlement core p08 test hermes slice descriptor pack
